item.title: Extract _find_prd and unify product/arch detection in init flow
item.intent: The PRD candidate list exists in one place. Product and architecture doc paths are detected once, not three times with three variable names. Init flow loses ~35 lines of copy-paste.
item.description: The same 10-element PRD candidate list (PRODUCT.md, product.md, PRD.md, prd.md, docs/ variants, WEBSITE_BRIEF.md, README.md) is copy-pasted verbatim in _init_detect_project, _init_scaffold_files, and cmd_backlog_populate. Product/arch existence is checked 3 times across the init flow with 3 different variable names (prd_found, product_found, found_product). Extract _find_prd function returning the path, use it everywhere. Detect product/arch docs once and pass paths through. Remove docs/WEBSITE_BRIEF.md from generic candidate list (project-specific leak).
steps: Create _find_prd function that returns the first matching PRD file path from the candidate list
Replace the candidate loop in _init_detect_project with _find_prd call
Replace the candidate loop in _init_scaffold_files with _find_prd call
Replace the candidate loop in cmd_backlog_populate with _find_prd call
Remove docs/WEBSITE_BRIEF.md from the candidate list
Unify product/arch detection: detect once in _init_detect_project, pass paths to scaffold and summary
Remove the re-detection loops in _init_scaffold_files and cmd_init summary
Verify: init -y and backlog populate both find the same docs as before
acceptanceCriteria: _find_prd function exists and is called from all 3 locations
No duplicate PRD candidate list exists in the script
docs/WEBSITE_BRIEF.md is not in the candidate list
Product/arch paths are detected once during init, not re-scanned
backlog populate finds the same PRD file as before
